events.title: US and Iran Near Peace Deal Framework
events.desc: US and Pakistani leaders have indicated that a framework agreement to end the conflict between the United States and Iran is expected to be signed on Sunday. While the US is optimistic about the timing, Tehran has expressed doubts about the timeline, and hardline protesters in Iran have voiced their opposition to the deal. The agreement is intended to bring an end to the ongoing war in the Middle East.

events.title: Israel Strikes Southern Lebanon
events.desc: Lebanon reported Israeli strikes in its southern and eastern regions on Saturday. The Israeli military issued evacuation warnings for Nabatieh and over twenty other locations in anticipation of raids. These strikes occurred amidst ongoing US-Iran negotiations for a potential Middle East peace deal that could include Lebanon, which was drawn into the conflict when Hezbollah attacked Israel in support of Iran.

events.title: UK PM Discusses Iran Conflict Resolution with US President
events.desc: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer spoke with U.S. President Donald Trump via phone regarding efforts to end the Iran conflict. Starmer welcomed the progress made and affirmed Britain's readiness to support the implementation of any peace agreement. Both leaders also emphasized the necessity of restoring freedom of navigation to alleviate global economic impacts.

events.title: US Strike Kills Sailors on Oil Tanker
events.desc: Three Indian sailors were killed when the US military attacked a Palau-flagged oil tanker on Wednesday. The US military stated the attack was in response to the tanker failing to comply with directions. The crew of the sanctioned tanker issued a distress call after it was struck by a missile, and they were later rescued off the coast of Oman.

events.title: US Strikes Iran After Helicopter Downing
events.desc: President Donald Trump accused Iran of shooting down a US military helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz and vowed retaliation. Subsequently, the US military conducted strikes against Iran in response. The incident and subsequent strikes heighten tensions in the region.

events.title: Israeli Air Strikes Hit Beirut Suburb
events.desc: Israel conducted air strikes on a Beirut suburb days after a US-brokered truce. The Israeli military stated that the attacks on the Lebanese capital were ordered in response to Hezbollah's firing into Israeli territory. This action risks undermining the recent ceasefire.

events.title: Israeli Strikes Kill 17 in Southern Lebanon
events.desc: Reports indicate that Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon have resulted in the deaths of 17 individuals. Nine of the casualties were reported in the town of Tayr Debba, according to Lebanon's state news agency. This escalation of violence follows recent diplomatic efforts to de-escalate tensions.

events.title: Two Killed in Israeli Strike on Gaza
events.desc: Palestinian authorities reported that an Israeli strike in southern Gaza resulted in the deaths of two people and injured another. This incident occurs amidst ongoing regional tensions and potential peace negotiations.

events.title: Iran and Israel Exchange Strikes Across Middle East
events.desc: For the second consecutive day, Iran and the United States have exchanged strikes across the Middle East. Iran claims to have responded to US strikes by targeting American assets in Kuwait, Jordan, and Bahrain. Bahrain reported damage to its capital city overnight.

events.title: US Military Infrastructure Targeted by Iranian Cyber Actors
events.desc: Iranian state-sponsored hacking groups have been observed conducting sophisticated cyber operations targeting critical US military infrastructure. These attacks aim to disrupt operations and gather intelligence, posing a significant threat to national security. The nature of the targets suggests a coordinated effort to degrade US military capabilities.

events.title: US-Iran Peace Deal Signing Expected Sunday, Strait of Hormuz to Open
events.desc: US President Donald Trump announced that a deal with Iran to end the war is scheduled for signing on Sunday, with the Strait of Hormuz to be immediately 'open to all' afterward. Iran has offered a different timeline but signaled an agreement is imminent. Mediators express optimism as negotiations conclude.

events.title: Iran's Police Infrastructure Targeted Amid Conflict
events.desc: Since the beginning of the conflict between the United States and Iran, at least 80 police stations and infrastructure related to law enforcement and the Basij paramilitary group have been damaged or destroyed. Experts suggest that both the US and Israel aim to degrade the Iranian regime's 'repressive capacity.'

events.title: US and Iran Exchange Strikes Across Middle East
events.desc: The US and Iran have engaged in retaliatory strikes across the Middle East for a second consecutive day. Iran claims to have targeted American assets in Kuwait, Jordan, and Bahrain in response to previous US strikes. Bahraini officials reported damage to its capital city during the night.

events.title: Iran claims funeral for Supreme Leader Khamenei set for July 4
events.desc: Iran's late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, who was reportedly killed in Israeli and US strikes in February, will have his funeral begin in Tehran on July 4, concluding with burial in Mashhad on July 9. This marks the end of his decades-long tenure as the leader of the Islamic Republic.
